---
title: "Events"
description: "Compare Solidity event declarations and emit calls with Move event structs and event::emit."
sidebar:
  label: "Events"
---

Move events serve the same product purpose as Solidity events: signal important on-chain state changes to off-chain readers.

## Solidity event

```solidity
event MessageAdded(address sender, string message, uint256 addedAt);
```

## Move event

```move
#[event]
struct MessageAdded has drop, store {
    sender: address,
    message: String,
    added_at: u64,
}
```

## Emitting the event

```solidity
emit MessageAdded(sender, message, block.timestamp);
```

```move
event::emit(MessageAdded {
    sender,
    message,
    added_at: timestamp::now_seconds(),
});
```

Move events are first-class structs. That gives you a cleaner typed model, and they can be queried later through the [Indexer](/build/indexer) or transaction APIs depending on the use case.
